findings.fix_prompt:
Remove the "Dev Bypass (Skip Login)" button, the "Choose Your Dev Role"
modal, and all "For development purposes only" copy from Sparkle Marketplace's
sign-in page. Only show these when import.meta.env.DEV is true. Never on the
published lovable.app URL.
findings.title: Anyone can skip login — dev bypass on the live URL
findings.what_we_saw:
Sparkle Marketplace's sign-in page ships with a "Dev Bypass (Skip Login)"
button. One click opens a role picker and drops visitors into the app with no
password.
findings.why_it_matters:
We catch this on most Lovable apps that shipped before a final "remove dev UI"
pass. On a marketplace, it means strangers can act as hosts or buyers.

findings.fix_prompt:
Remove the "Developer Tools" / "Development Data Seeder" section from
Sparkle's signed-in homepage. Gate the Seed and Clear data buttons behind
import.meta.env.DEV only.
findings.title: "Developer Tools" panel on the homepage customers see
findings.what_we_saw:
After sign-in, Sparkle's homepage shows a "Developer Tools" card with
"Seed Sample Data" and "Clear My Data" — visible on the public URL, not hidden
behind a dev flag.
findings.why_it_matters:
Paying users and investors read this as "still in beta." It undercuts every
polished pixel around it.

findings.fix_prompt:
Add privacy and terms routes with basic policy content for Sparkle Marketplace.
Link both from the footer on every page. Use your real support email in the
contact section. Match the rest of Sparkle's typography and spacing.
findings.title: Privacy and Terms links go to 404
findings.what_we_saw:
Sparkle's footer promises legal pages, but both privacy and terms URLs return
the app's 404 screen.
findings.why_it_matters:
Stripe, App Store reviewers, and B2B buyers all look for these before they
trust you with money or data. Missing pages are an instant credibility hit.

findings.fix_prompt:
Fix Quick Book on Sparkle Marketplace so available slots appear, OR hide
Quick Book when no slots exist and show "No dates available yet, check back soon."
findings.title: Hero "Quick Book" CTA dead-ends — zero bookable slots
findings.what_we_saw:
Sparkle's main "Quick Book" button opens a modal that says "No available slots
found" and keeps "Book Now" disabled. There is no fallback path to book.
findings.why_it_matters:
This is the conversion path you designed the whole app around. Every visitor
who tries to book hits a wall — and most won't email you about it.

findings.fix_prompt:
On Sparkle's Quick Book modal, constrain height to the viewport, make the slot
list scroll inside the modal, and pin "Book Now" above the safe area on mobile.
Test on iPhone SE and 390px width in Chrome DevTools.
findings.title: Booking modal breaks on mobile — CTA below the fold
findings.what_we_saw:
At phone width, Sparkle's Quick Book modal is taller than the screen. The time
picker and "Book Now" button sit below the fold with no obvious scroll.
findings.why_it_matters:
Most vibe-coded launches get more than half their traffic on phones. A broken
mobile booking flow is silent revenue loss.

findings.fix_prompt:
On Sparkle listing cards, when the review count is 0, show "New" or "No reviews
yet" instead of a numeric rating. Only show the star number once at least one
real review exists.
findings.title: Listings show 4.5 stars with (0) reviews
findings.what_we_saw:
Sparkle experience cards display "4.5 (0)" — a star rating with zero reviews
behind it.
findings.why_it_matters:
Buyers know what seeded demo data looks like. It reads as fake social proof
and makes real hosts look worse by association.

findings.fix_prompt:
On Sparkle Marketplace, replace "Hosted by Local Host" with each experience's
actual host display name from the database. If missing, show first name + last
initial or hide the line.
findings.title: Every listing still says "Hosted by Local Host"
findings.what_we_saw: All Sparkle listings use the same placeholder host name instead of real people.
findings.why_it_matters:
Marketplaces live or die on trust in the person behind the listing. Placeholder
copy tells buyers the inventory isn't real yet.

passed_checks: Search, filters, and listing detail pages feel production-ready
Core branding and layout are ahead of most pre-launch vibe-coded apps
Auth and navigation work once dev-only UI is removed

share_metadata.description: Sample Starter audit for Sparkle Marketplace: 2 critical launch-stoppers, 3 high-priority trust and mobile issues — the kind we catch on Lovable apps every week.
share_metadata.title: LaunchLook audit for Sparkle Marketplace

verdict.label: Needs fixes before launch
verdict.narrative:
Sparkle Marketplace looks investor-ready in screenshots — then you click
through. Dev shortcuts are still on the live URL, legal pages 404, the hero
booking CTA fails, and mobile checkout is effectively broken. None of this
requires a redesign: these are the high-frequency fixes we ship on Starter audits
for Lovable and Cursor apps. Clear the two critical items and the booking path
before you share the link publicly; the rest is trust polish you can batch this
week.
verdict.summary: Polished shell, three launch-stoppers we'd fix before you post the URL anywhere.
